name: Paneer Butter Masala
healthFacts: Paneer adds ~14g protein per 100g — a near-complete source for vegetarians.
Cashews thicken the gravy without flour — adds magnesium + healthy fats.
Tomatoes deliver lycopene, an antioxidant that's MORE bioavailable when cooked.
Kasuri methi (dried fenugreek) is linked to better blood sugar response.
Swap cream for cashew paste to keep richness while cutting saturated fat by ~40%.
Ginger aids digestion — especially handy with rich, dairy-forward dishes.

steps.title: Soak the cashews
steps.detail: Drop cashews in ½ cup hot water. Let them sit while you prep — they need to soften so the gravy stays silky, not gritty.
steps.tip: Hot tap water works. Boiling = 5 min. Room temp = 30 min.

steps.title: Sauté onion + tomato
steps.detail: Roughly chop onion and tomatoes. In 1 tbsp butter, sauté onion for 3 min until translucent, then add tomatoes, ginger-garlic paste, and chili. Cook 6–7 min until tomatoes break down completely.
steps.tip: Don't rush this. Tomatoes should look jammy and oil should separate slightly.

steps.title: Blend the gravy base
steps.detail: Cool slightly, then blend the onion-tomato mix with the soaked cashews and ½ cup water until completely smooth. Pass through a fine strainer back into the pan — this is what makes restaurant-style silky.
steps.tip: Skip the strainer and you'll get a rustic, homestyle texture. Both are valid.

steps.title: Bloom the spices
steps.detail: Add 2 tbsp butter to the pan. Once melted, add red chili powder and stir for 10 seconds — just long enough to bloom, not burn. Pour the strained gravy back in.
steps.tip: Kashmiri chili is mild + gives the signature red color without heat.

steps.title: Simmer the gravy
steps.detail: Add salt, sugar, and ¼ cup water if needed. Simmer covered on low for 8 minutes, stirring once or twice. The gravy should gloss up and reduce slightly.
steps.tip: Sugar isn't for sweetness — it balances tomato acidity. Don't skip.

steps.title: Add paneer + finish
steps.detail: Cube paneer (1-inch). Slip it into the gravy, add cream, garam masala, and crushed kasuri methi. Simmer 2 more minutes. Don't stir aggressively — paneer breaks easily.
steps.tip: Soak paneer cubes in warm water for 10 min beforehand for extra-soft texture.

steps.title: Rest, then serve
steps.detail: Turn off the heat and let it sit covered for 5 minutes. The flavors marry in this window. Garnish with a swirl of cream and a pinch of methi. Serve with naan or jeera rice.
steps.tip: This rest step is what separates 'good' from 'great'. Try it.
